# Extracao: transcription_mp4_01-CBO_Mastery

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_01-CBO_Mastery.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0.03 MB

---

The CBO method is based on structure, process and repeatable results. Now when CBO campaign budget optimization came out sometime in 2017, I didn't embrace it initially. I was a bit confused as to why they'd introduce it and we just didn't really look at it for about six months and it was just something which as Facebook do they introduce many tools but it's difficult to know what you should focus on so story ads come out should I run story ads. Campaign budget optimization comes out should I run that. And it wasn't something we touched until we had a bit more time to start playing around with testing. It was introduced as a way of taking out some of the stress and burden of managing multiple budgets across multiple ad sets. So some people like myself said at the time I like that control, I want to be in control, it doesn't work for me. Now if I kind of look at where CBO has taken us right now in terms of the ad accounts we run and essentially the BPM method, CBO has become a central part of everything we do. There are some key things that it introduces but I think that a lot of people don't really understand how to use it and therefore they either avoid it or they hate it. Now for me CBO brings five key things. Number one is stability. Because of the machine learning inside of CBO, if you learn how the algorithm works and we've talked a lot about the algorithm in the BPM method and you understand all the inputs of creating stability which is a big part of the BPM method then you'll be able to use CBO to your advantage. So CBO is not just a way of firing up a campaign with lots of ad sets and lots of ads and just hoping that it works, it just does not work that way. You have to have the inputs in place to be able to get that performance. CBO also gives us better structure. So the way that we actually manage our different types of audiences, our audience sizes, it just fits in and fits in perfectly to everything we do. The four funnel system, the way we set up different audiences from lookalikes, interests, etc. But the other thing that it really brings is efficiency and we've run many tests where we've tried campaign budget optimization versus ad set budgets for example. Back in 2018 we were testing this quite a lot and we found that although there was perhaps a bit more turbulence and unexpected results in the first 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 days for example, once a CBO had reached stability meaning that we had enough conversions and the results were steady it remained steadier for far, far, far longer than ad set budgets and that just blew me away because at that point in 2018 we were heavily relying on rules and automation so we would be doing lots of scaling and optimization and much of it was driven based on the rules that we had created but the problem with having too many rules is it's easy to lose track of exactly what's going on and CBO introduced a level of efficiency that even to this date we don't use a tenth of the rules that we used to because CBO manages a lot of that decision making and that comes through the intelligence of the CBO engine. Now I don't know what that code looks like inside but I'm pretty sure it's a complex beast because what it does is you're not just literally lifting your budgets from ad sets to your campaign level you're also giving that campaign visibility on far more than it had at the ad set level so if you have a $500 budget at the campaign level and five ad sets that campaign now manages five ad sets it doesn't manage $100 per ad set it manages five ad set and tries to get you the best results and that's a key part of you know what Facebook are trying to introduce and get people to understand with CBO and this is why we appreciate it so much and it brings a level of calm to our media buying so prior to CBO the BPM method went a long way to allowing us to have better control understand what was going on reporting analysis and just be able to take better control of our performance but CBO has brought a different level of calm and you'll see that as you go through the BPM method and apply each part of the structure using CBO you'll see that come through in your ad account as well. Now taking a step back here prior to 2014 there was a setup in Facebook as you can see here in the bottom right where all there was was a campaign and ads there was no concept of ad sets and actually the budget was managed at the campaign level so prior to 2014 the concept of CBO existed there just wasn't the concept of an ad set so your targeting and everything was at the ad level it was chaos it was absolutely difficult to work with at the time and you know for anyone that was around back then you could you couldn't access ads manager without a chrome plugin which was quite crazy as well but at the time in 2014 is when I first scaled an e-com store to eight million dollars and a big part of this was campaign structure so at the time I had already spent a lot of time and money on paid search but it took me 12 to 18 months to really figure out Facebook ads because I went from the mindset of high intent keywords on Google search so if I'm bidding on something like 42 inch LCD TV I know the intent someone's looking to buy and in my mind I was still focusing very much on really really kind of high intent direct response advertising on Facebook but that didn't quite work and you know what I tried to do was to restructure my thought process based on keeping audiences and ads locked in and what I mean by that is if there's a specific audience that you know may resonate better with one ad than another then I'd focus on that and that's a big part of what created the graduation testing process which is finding audience and ad matches so back back in kind of 2014 as I started to figure things out that was one of the things I started to introduce and it kind of you know back then prior to that I was kind of doing what a lot of Facebook marketers still do in terms of micro ad sets so spreading one dollar five dollar budgets into many many ad sets seeing what worked and pausing the bad performers this was back in 2013 2014 that I was doing it and you know unfortunately some people still do that today it was a way of hacking so you know you just throw things at the wall and see what would work the problem over time is that as you're growing a business like there's a difference between spending money on ads and generating some money and actually growing a business I'm more interested in growing businesses so my structures for my agency and for my own e-com and digital products are all about building consistency with that predictability because I want to grow a business and those short-term hacks just don't allow you to do that and you know one of the things is about introducing the right ads at the right time into your ad sets and the new structure that Facebook introduced in 2014 enabled us to do that a lot better so that was a big big step forward and then the budgets were moved into ad set levels and you know since 2014 that became the norm then in 2017 Facebook said well we're going to go back to campaign budgets and you know that that was I think ignored by so many people as well but when I went into the cbo engine and I kind of started to understand what Facebook were trying to do things started to make a lot more sense and you know since 2018 I've gone through and spent literally like I don't know five ten million overall on all the ad accounts not just testing cbo but scaling it as well and and you know what what I found was this illustration for from Facebook is exactly what happens with cbo so on the left hand side you've got the typical ad set budget setup so each ad set is allocated ten dollars because you know you're setting that yourself and each ad set therefore works independently of each other so ad set one ad set two ad set three in essence are competitors so they all have their own ten dollar budgets and they're going to compete for performance now that they may or may not compete in the audience that's a completely separate thing but as far as your budget and your spend goes all three ads ad sets are working independently to try and get you the best performance the campaign level has no control over that so it's just kind of passive in terms of just being a container so ad set one in this example has spent ten dollars and generated three conversions ad set two has spent ten dollars also and generated five conversions and ad set three is spent ten dollars generate two conversions so overall you've spent thirty dollars and got ten conversions so you know that that's okay but the the problem here and the problem that Facebook tried to solve is what happened if we took that same thirty dollars but let let us decide how to spend it and get you the best performance so on the right hand side same budget of thirty dollars is now set at the campaign level and the campaign is now monitoring each of the ad set so it's like introducing a new manager where previously your kind of three members of the team were all doing their own separate media buying now the manager is saying to each of the three different media buyers show me good performance and i'll give you the budget to spend so the the manager in this case the campaign budget optimized campaign is saying if your performance is good i'll give you this budget and if it's not i'll move it across somewhere else that will give me a better return and and that's essentially what's happening and it's happening in real time so you might look at the ad set based budgets and say well i can have rules and automation and i can increase and decrease spend etc etc you absolutely can but can you do that in real time can you do that at the auction level as your ad is getting entered to decide where where that spend goes which ad sets get pushed forward which ads get pushed forward you can't and and that's the power of cbo that is doing things that you can't see and this is also one of the challenges with cbo is of understanding what's going on in the background because sometimes it feels like cbo's not doing what you want it to do but it's actually testing and optimizing far more than you can see as well so in the example on the right you can see the spend levels so the three different ad sets are very different the ad set two has actually generated far more conversions and has taken the bulk of the budget and actually overall you now have 15 conversions instead of 10 so it's the same spend but you've actually got more conversions so you might kind of look at that and say yeah that's all fine in theory we we said the same until we tested it and we saw exactly what facebook were describing so in this example here we ran a campaign with three audiences the three audiences were exactly the same so you know it wasn't as if any of the audiences or the ads were going to have any kind of difference on performance exactly the same audiences set up configuration exactly the same ads running in each as well and we ran this for I think about maybe a week and what we wanted to do with this was to kind of find out what would happen if we just let it run like this is one of the things that we see that you know facebook will tell you to do is to just run the budget and monitor the results and actually what came back was initially there was a challenge around the data so the first kind of day wasn't so good second date started to get better and then actually what we found was that the campaign performance overall at the campaign level we were getting a $35 $36 cost per acquisition and for us that was well within our target but interestingly at the ad set level and I think we were spending maybe $250 $300 a day at the ad set level the spend was being managed based on performance and as you can see from the highlight here the ad set that had the best CPA was also getting the most spend the ad set with the worst CPA was getting the lowest spend and then you can see the one in between that and that was perfect that that was a big moment for me to kind of realize yes you know this CBO thing is actually doing exactly what it should be doing and that built up a ton of confidence to kind of spread this out and go wider you know this is about giving giving up some control to facebook I know some of you will be really you know listening to this and looking at the material on CBO and saying I don't want to give up that control I don't trust facebook to do that but if you understand and we'll go through in the next few screens on the inputs into CBO and actually how to make that work for you it will give you the return you're looking for in terms of taking away some of the stress on the kind of day-to-day management of CBO and allow you to get that stability in the calm that you you want from it as well because essentially CBO is about for me it's about taking out the stress of the day-to-day and allowing you to spend more time as per the whole BPM method program on your ad campaigns and this this example here is looking at the full funnel system and this is kind of how our CBO campaigns generally look we have our four funnels we have our four funnel parts we have our budgets set at the highest level and to an extent with with some kind of general control we let CBO run its budgets and we let that performance come in some of you will be running on small budgets now when you look at CBO and some of the case studies out there a lot of people have spent big money and made it work CBO absolutely works better with bigger spends however one of the triggers back in 2018 that got me to look into CBO was actually reading a case study from Facebook on a small business out in South America I think it was that implemented CBO and had huge success they weren't spending a lot I think it was like 250 dollars a day but they switched their optimization from seven-day click to one-day click and they saw massive improvement in results so if you've read any of my material on CBO or heard me talk about CBO generally I will always favor a one-day click and it depends on the average order value of the item and how long it takes people to buy but that one change when we implemented it we saw the same result and that's one of the things about the small CBO budgets is that get the configuration right and it can work so firstly one of the options around you know if you really want to run CBO in the same way as ad set budgets is you'd have a single campaign with a single ad set for all intents and purposes is the same as running your budgets at the ad set level the downside is that the CBO engine cannot I think cope with not having enough variables so if you're just running the one ad set then generally it doesn't perform as well and as you saw in the previous example if all you've got is one ad set then duplicate it at least two and ideally three times we do this often when we find a winning audience if all we've got is that one winning audience we duplicate it two or three times actually Facebook because of the way the auction works and the way the kind of buckets work within the different audiences and how your ads drop into different parts of the auction and that starts to build up the data the ad set level just simply duplicating that single ad set in your campaign two or three times even if that's all you've got can make a big difference on performance as well the second part of small budget consideration is spend versus time so if you don't have a big budget so you're spending $50 a day for example it's going to take you longer for stability and to get the results you want so if that's the case then the next part is minimizing variables so you cannot run a CBO at $50 a day with five ad sets and five ads running because that's 25 variables so the five ad sets and the five ads plus all the variables of age and gender and everything else within that your $50 won't go far it'll probably take your whole month to get significant results so what do you do you reduce the variables you might run two ad tests you might run two ads in each therefore your variables down to four in terms of ad sets and ads so that's how I would balance out the smaller budgets also the conversion path makes a big difference so if you're optimizing for a registration and your journey is add landing page register those small budgets will get conversion data faster and actually stabilize faster as well if you're doing a typical ecom journey or click add a few product add to cart start check out in purchase and you're spending small numbers then optimizing for purchase won't give you as stable results as optimizing for something like add to cart so that's just something to consider and that's a big part of the graduation testing process to find the right pixel event as well use min and max budgets so you can set minimum spend and maximum spend limits at the ad set level which for smaller budgets can be a bit more useful as well and then you've also got lifetime budgets now the next screen i'm going to show is actually showing you how lifetime budgets work for smaller spend campaigns as well and then final part is the bpm method like you absolutely need to have a process in place to fill your cbo's with good quality audiences ads and pixel data to give you stability as well so talking about small spend cbo's this is an account that we are running and what this account is you know if you look at the stats on this account there's four months of data there so month one was a spend of 900 dollars that was i believe about 30 dollars a day give or take we took that 30 dollars a day and generated the romance of 2.74 which for for them was just slightly off target we're aiming for three but 2.74 return on ad spend with a 30 dollars a day spend was pretty good the following month we upped our budgets ever so slightly hit a 2.01 and then we almost doubled our budgets and also increased roas and then increased budget again in month four and this is by using small budgets but we were using lifetime budgets so the way that we pitch up lifetime budgets is to create the campaign so you'd set your budget at the campaign level but you'd select lifetime budget instead of your daily budget so for example if you're looking to spend 30 dollars a day you would potentially set your budget at let's say 600 dollars and run it for 20 days for example so that's roughly 30 dollars a day then what you do is monitor it as it's going along and facebook will spend based on performance if the if you can spend more and get great results it'll spend more than 30 dollars and likewise it will spend less as well it's something to experiment and play with and it's essentially what we found was that over that period of time the performance maintained and stabilized and what we would do is one week ahead of that kind of period ending so if we're running a campaign for 20 days then about five to seven days before that period ended would extend the campaign date for the lifetime budget and increase the budget again and that's that's all we did on this account now we couldn't scale up because this was a small local business that were constrained by operations and things like that so despite the fact that return on ad spend has been so good we can't scale it up beyond kind of what they're doing the problem with that is if you can't scale up then sometimes it leads to instability but with lifetime budgets we're able to keep that going so that's a key consideration it's worth testing i'm not saying that lifetime budgets is the absolute solution for small budgets but it's a consideration for you to test as well the other part of cbo so you know once you've understood cbo and you set up your campaigns one thing to kind of think about is what facebook are trying to do which i agree with is to move people away from spending so much time in ads manager and media buying and spend more time on your custom experience and your advertising so the abcd formula is based on creating a perfect balance within your ad account so for example auto placements and bidding big audiences cbo and dynamic content optimization so in one of the other videos as you'll see we ran dynamic content optimize ads for our top of funnel cold audiences on broad using auto placements and auto bidding and that beat everything that beat our lookalikes our interests we even did manual bidding testing as well and it beat all of those out because we went through the structure of setting up the bpm method we primed the pixel really well with our graduation testing and our optimization and everything else that went into it to the extent that the the top performing campaign actually didn't need us at all like we set it up with broad camp it was a broad campaign auto placements auto bidding cbo setup and dynamic content optimization which meant that facebook was doing all the hard work for us like it was insane to look at this and come from a background of performance marketing and media buying to give that control to facebook but it can absolutely work and you'll see that through this program as well so then if you now tailor the the setup and add the bpm method to the abcd formula what does that look like what it's actually the perfect balance in terms of your campaign structure combined with a perfect advertising system so this is a big thing about why the bpm method and cbo is so married is because what I want for you is to take out the stress and anxiety of running your day to day ads obviously there's a big effort that you're going to be putting in now but combined with cbo and everything else that we're taking you through in the bpm method it brings that stability and structure to enable you to take a step back so this is perfect for big audiences so you might look at that and say well well that small audiences well there's a solution for that as well the acds formula aka the dco dpa infinity retargeting strategy which you'll see in the optimization section dco dynamic content optimized ads dpa are the dynamic products ads and what that allows you to do is for smaller audiences so for me the warm and hot audiences when you've got this setup with small audience windows just like a waterfall the audiences keep pouring in and they refresh so if you're retargeting people using a dynamic product ads over seven days after their site visit then every seven days that audience refreshes in fact every day it refreshes and once you've got your ads and your messages dialed in and your optimizations it literally becomes a set and forget and you'll see that in the kind of retargeting strategies so just to kind of recap on the cbo setup there's 10 keys to cbo success number one is time spend and patience if you have high budgets you can get you can accelerate your results you don't have high budgets then it's going to take you longer to get your results but you need to work on stability with a few variables cbo needs patience so sometimes we monitor our cbo's really closely on day one day two three day three but we don't run rules then we let it kind of let the algorithm give us the feedback on what's working and what's not and generally we see after two three days that performance starts to come in and as you've seen previously it's the cbo algorithm that's finding us the best conversions across those ad sets so it does require a level of patience I myself sometimes get a bit anxious and you know I want to pause the ads and sometimes my team remind me hey you create a training course and you say you shouldn't touch it absolutely right follow the process and you know it's it's hard for sometimes the best of us to follow that same process but the process exists especially for us because we test it we test everything thoroughly before you guys see anything so for everything that I'm sharing with you we probably tested 50 or 100 things that didn't work as well but the things that work are strictly down to process you follow the process and they work higher budgets give you faster stability but higher spend gives you high stability and what I mean by that is if you're spending a thousand dollars a day in your campaign obviously you'll get stability faster if things are performing well but higher spend over a period of time also build stability so if you're spending $50 a day but you've built that stability all the way through your campaign as time goes by you'll create even more stability meaning that your performance becomes more predictable as well the funnel stage matters for stability and spend so if you're at top of the funnel one of the things that we like to do and you'll see this through the testing and prospecting stage is to group our campaigns together by size and quality and also kind of where you are in the cold warm hot funnel then drives the way you set up your budgets and ad sets as well in general we don't go more than five to seven ad sets per campaign with three to four ads running per ad set as well and this really depends on the spend levels and maturity the scaling etc but going beyond that it just introduces too many variables into cbo where it just starts to destabilize we use min and max budgets for some spend control especially at the testing stage so if you're doing testing and you know you've got a hundred dollar budget across five ad sets for example we might set a ten dollar min spend at the different ad set levels so each of those ad sets get tested and that will be something we cover in the graduation testing section we're also pre-testing ads and ad sets so I've already talked about the fact that cbo is a great engine and it works but it isn't simply a case of just throwing ads at the wall and seeing what works doing the research ahead of time makes a huge difference and the final final part of making cbo's work just like any advertising campaign is having great advertising so cbo is not silver bullet that is going to enable you to massively profit and scale and and sit back and you know just literally print money out of facebook it's just one of the enablers it's a cog in a bigger machine but if you get that machine working perfectly then cbo will literally print your cash
